description:
A Solar Energy Installation Manager has completed a site survey of a commercial warehouse in Phoenix, AZ. The flat-pitched roof has two viable mounting areas: a larger south-facing section and a smaller west-facing section.

Using PySAM's detailed PV model (`Pvsamv1`), model a ~100 kW-DC commercial rooftop PV system split across these two subarrays:
- **Subarray 1 (South)**: ~60 kW DC capacity, 180° azimuth, 15° tilt, fixed roof mount, 0.4 ground coverage ratio.
- **Subarray 2 (West)**: ~40 kW DC capacity, 270° azimuth, 10° tilt, fixed roof mount, 0.4 ground coverage ratio.

Additional Requirements:
- **Location**: Phoenix, AZ (find and use a bundled Phoenix TMY weather file from SAM's solar_resource directory).
- **Equipment**: Use default CEC modules and inverters from a commercial PV default configuration (e.g., 'FlatPlatePVCommercial').
- **Inverter Sizing**: Size the system with a DC-to-AC ratio of approximately 1.2 (~83 kW AC).
- **Financial**: Run the Single Owner financial model (`PySAM.Singleowner`) chained to your PV model to calculate the real LCOE.

Save the complete simulation results as a JSON file exactly at:
`/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/multi_subarray_results.json`

The JSON must have this exact structure:
{
  "location": "Phoenix, AZ",
  "weather_file": "<filename>",
  "system": { "total_dc_capacity_kw": 100.0, "total_ac_capacity_kw": 83.3, "dc_ac_ratio": 1.2 },
  "subarray1": { "dc_capacity_kw": 60.0, "azimuth_deg": 180, "tilt_deg": 15, "tracking": "fixed", "annual_energy_kwh": <value> },
  "subarray2": { "dc_capacity_kw": 40.0, "azimuth_deg": 270, "tilt_deg": 10, "tracking": "fixed", "annual_energy_kwh": <value> },
  "annual_energy_kwh": <total_kwh>,
  "capacity_factor_pct": <value>,
  "lcoe_real_cents_per_kwh": <value>
}
